item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the least dangerous SCP I've seen. Worst case: u solve an IRL crime

item.title: Made a small edit that might help things.
item.content:encoded:
Been banging my head against the wall for the better part of six months trying to figure out how to do a better ending to this article. Realized a couple nights ago that giving away the ending in the containment procedures probably led to a greatly lessened impact, and that perhaps instead of rewriting the ending I could just take that bit out of the containment procedures.

Everything else I could think of kind of clashed with the tone I had in mind so this is probably what I'm settling on. Want to think about another skip now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh, sometimes items don't have to go anywhere special, they just need to be weird. Case in point, SCP-527, another Wondertainment product. Plenty of things cranked out by Dr. W are just odd and anomalous, but ultimately harmless.

I wouldn't be averse to this being expanded a little though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Starts with an interesting concept but doesn't feel like it really goes anywhere with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I too feel like the concept of this SCP was excellent, but needs a little something more to make it truly great. Also, two questions about wording -

The building was completed lacking a front wall, and the access materials were put to the side of the structure.

Access, or excess?

three day old fresh bread

How is it fresh if it's three days old?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had actually upvoted at some point during the description, because I loved the premise of this, but I was ultimately let down at the events portrayed in the logs, which did not seem to go much of anywhere. Really hoping this gets revisited, reluctant downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, mock murder mystery seems right up Dr. Wondertainment's alley, and this a rather interesting way of answering the prompt.

I do agree with M_E that the ending was a little lackluster, as it just kind of drops off, but the rest of the document is able to secure my vote.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
[The victim] has never been documented to be SCP-3585-D.

Methinks the deer is a serial deerial killer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can't say Wondertainment ever crossed my mind while thinking of ideas for the Murder Mystery theme, so props on that. Funnily enough, it's the cute moments that really make this work for me. +1

I wonder if there's any reason why -D seems to be in charge of the proceedings?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was supposed to be a kind of heartwarming moment, but I can certainly see how it doesn't feel super climactic. Might consider changing the ending once the contest is over to better state what I was going for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was enjoying this up until the end, which instead of a climax just repeated info previously told to us about how successful guesses go.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love Dr. Wondertainment items so you already got me hooked. +1

item.title: Murder Mystery Time!
item.content:encoded:
Thought that it would be fun to do a somewhat light hearted article considering this topic might not inspire a ton of them.

Thanks to Stormbreath in the chat for giving me crit on this!

I hope you all enjoy!
